refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve the customer-defined teleoperation link availability target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was mostly atomic but slightly ambiguous regarding the evaluation period and the nature of the availability target. Refining it to explicitly include the evaluation period and framing it as a target clarifies the requirement without implying any solution or technology.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the teleoperation link availability over the agreed evaluation period and verify that it meets or exceeds the customer-defined target (e.g., ≥ 0.999).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall achieve the customer-defined teleoperation link availability target over the agreed evaluation period.

candidate_dps: Implement redundant communication paths to ensure failover capability
Incorporate real-time link health monitoring and adaptive error correction
Design a robust link management protocol with automatic recovery and reconnection
Establish a predictive maintenance system for communication hardware and software
Utilize dynamic bandwidth allocation and prioritization to maintain link quality
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement redundant communication paths to ensure failover capability

potential_sources: Tight integration between monitoring and recovery mechanisms
coupling_check.notes: Selecting redundant communication paths as the DP promotes independence by isolating failure domains and enabling failover without requiring complex interdependencies. Other candidate DPs may introduce coupling due to their reliance on real-time data exchange and adaptive control loops.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that redundant paths can be physically and logically separated to avoid common mode failures
Risk that redundancy increases system complexity and cost
Assumes operational policies allow for failover mechanisms without violating safety plans
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining the evaluation period and environmental conditions in the FR to better tailor DP selection and risk assessment.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall communication infrastructure and network management components responsible for implementing communication protocols and failover mechanisms.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles the physical and logical network connections, enabling redundant communication paths and failover.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends status and health data over the network, relying on the redundant communication paths.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands and tasks that require reliable communication channels.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as NGHE Solution manages communication infrastructure; minimal diffusion as failover is core to network management.
Vehicle module uses communication but does not manage redundancy; would cause diffusion of network logic.
Teleoperation depends on communication but does not implement redundancy; partial containment but some diffusion.
Safety Services may use communication but is not responsible for network redundancy; poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing redundant communication paths because it directly manages network infrastructure and failover mechanisms, ensuring high cohesion and low diffusion of the DP.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle or Teleoperation modules if they implement their own communication redundancy, leading to duplication.
Interface complexity with multiple modules requiring access to the redundant communication paths may increase coupling.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Implement redundant communication paths to ensure failover capability' directly addresses the FR of achieving the customer-defined teleoperation link availability target by providing a logical solution to maintain communication availability through redundancy and failover.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle or Teleoperation modules if they implement their own communication redundancy, leading to duplication.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high cohesion and low diffusion scores, indicating it is the appropriate boundary for implementing the DP. Minimal diffusion risk exists as failover is core to network management and is centralized within this module.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible by measuring the link availability over the agreed evaluation period to ensure it meets or exceeds the customer-defined target (≥ 0.999). The NGHE Solution module's interfaces support monitoring and control necessary for verification.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear, with well-defined interfaces for network link, telemetry, and tasking that support the implementation and verification of redundant communication paths.

recommended_revisions: Clarify interface contracts to prevent other modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation) from implementing redundant communication independently to avoid diffusion and duplication.
Establish clear ownership and responsibilities in the architecture documentation to reinforce that NGHE Solution is solely responsible for redundancy and failover mechanisms.
Consider defining stricter interface boundaries or access controls to reduce coupling and complexity arising from multiple modules accessing the redundant communication paths.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current level of decomposition is sufficient. The DP of implementing redundant communication paths adequately addresses the FR, and the allocation to the NGHE Solution module is clear with manageable diffusion risks. Verification is feasible and ownership is well defined. Although some recommended revisions exist to clarify interface contracts and ownership to prevent diffusion and duplication, these do not necessitate further decomposition at this stage. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and centralized implementation is preferable to avoid complexity.

revise.why: To clarify interface contracts and ownership responsibilities to prevent diffusion and duplication of redundancy mechanisms across modules, thereby reducing coupling and complexity.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module is capable of fully implementing and verifying the redundant communication paths.
Other modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation) will comply with interface contracts and ownership boundaries once clarified.
open_risks: Potential overlap or duplication of redundancy mechanisms if interface contracts are not enforced.
Complexity arising from multiple modules accessing redundant communication paths if access controls are insufficient.
evidence_needed: Verification data showing link availability meets or exceeds 0.999 over evaluation period.
Documentation of interface contracts and ownership responsibilities confirming clarity and enforcement.
